stem: A retail study measures customer satisfaction score across 3 chains, each containing multiple stores. chain A has 10 observations with mean 74.1 /10 (within-chain SD = 5.5). chain B has 8 observations with mean 69.4 /10 (within-chain SD = 7.9). chain C has 10 observations with mean 60.6 /10 (within-chain SD = 9.2). The between-chain standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 4.6 and 11.6 /10. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true customer satisfaction score for chain B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-chain variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for chain B: 69.2 /10 (shrunk from raw 69.4). Range [69.0, 69.3] as between-chain SD varies from 4.6 to 11.6. Pooled mean: 67.9.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 67.9 /10 (weighted average of 3 chain means).
Shrinkage factor for chain B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=7.7: B = 0.884.
Interval [69.0, 69.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (69.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (67.9) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
